reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All gates passed. Gate 3 (Code Verification): Phase 03's code reading is factually correct - BlobsByRange protocol is added to supported_protocols at L396-400 based solely on fork_exists(Deneb), not epoch; handler at L992-1101 only validates data_availability_boundary (pruning), no deprecation logic for FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS exists. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e entry PROP-6a4369e9-inv-028 (outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json L770-786) requires 'blob_sidecars_by_range must be fully deprecated after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS' with assertion 'current_epoch >= FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS => respond_empty_or_reject'. Code deviates by continuing to serve requests indefinitely. An attacker can trigger this via P2P by sending BlobsByRange requests after the deprecation deadline, forcing nodes to maintain dual serving obligations (blobs + data columns) instead of fully migrating to PeerDAS, causing resource burden and bypassing intended migration path. Verdict is CONFIRMED_POTENTIAL rather than CONFIRMED_VULNERABILITY because while the spec deviation is clear (missing epoch-based deprecation), the practical exploitability depends on whether the resource burden from dual serving creates sufficient impact. Medium severity is justified per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json (affects resource management and protocol migration, not direct validator slashing or network split).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: blob_sidecars_by_range must be fully deprecated after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS. (assertion: current_epoch >= FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS => respond_empty_or_reject)
